codeReview.summary: The evidence-only revalidation feature stays within scope and correctly repairs the ownership chain for the already-landed RecordId roundtrip table-identity fix. The handoff, transcript, and review artifact consistently point to the real runtime implementation in commit f6cbcd5 for requested RecordId reinjection in Repo::get_record and to the focused proof commit 4aec035 plus rerun targeted regressions for reload/delete behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker procedure is still framed around implementation features and does not clearly cover evidence-only revalidation work where the task is to repair ownership/audit artifacts for already-landed behavior rather than add failing tests or new runtime code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The transcript skeleton and handoff for worker session 795a1137-5b52-4753-bd62-de2032f10a17 mark followedProcedure=false only because the session audited existing commits and reran focused regressions instead of performing fail-first implementation steps.

summary: Pass: the feature-owned evidence chain now correctly ties the existing get_record RecordId table-identity implementation to commit f6cbcd5 and the focused reload/delete proof to commit 4aec035 plus successful targeted reruns, resolving the prior mis-owned evidence gap.
